title: People are marrying holograms and making friends with chatbots. But can AI bring true happiness?
description: Can technology really replace human relationships? As philosophy scholars who focus on human happiness and on artificial intelligence (AI), we tackle this question in a recent paper. In our study, we address the rise of AI companions, chatbots, and social robots for friendship, advice, emotional support, and even romance. We…
content:

title: China Is Taking Its AI Boom Under the Sea
description: China is pioneering underwater and offshore data center technologies as governments and companies seek greener ways to support the rapidly growing computing demands of artificial intelligence.
content: The data centre industry has taken off in the last few years, as tech companies look to develop the massive computing power needed to run complex operations, such as artificial intelligence. Thousands of data centres are being developed on land, but ... [5282 chars]

title: Trump Says AI Is Bigger Than Internet, Defends Scrutiny of Anthropic
description: President Donald Trump said artificial intelligence will have a greater impact than the internet.
content:
President Donald Trump said artificial intelligence will have a greater impact than the internet and defended his administration's scrutiny of AI companies, including Anthropic.
"It's bigger than the internet," Trump said in an interview with Axios.

title: Va. job market faces AI challenge: high-achieving college grads ‘having trouble getting jobs’
description: Artificial intelligence has prompted some employers to replace highly skilled employees in high-tech industries with computer programs that excel at performing many of the routine tasks of software engineers, which has impacted recent college graduates.

title: Recto: PH to fast-track incentives for AI, semicon investors

title: AI adoption outpaces institutions -- Stanford study
description: ARTIFICIAL intelligence is being adopted across education, healthcare, workplaces and consumer services faster than institutions can adapt, according to the 2026 AI Index Report released by the Stanford Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence (HAI), which found growing use of AI technologies alongside mounting concerns over governance, transparency and workforce disruption.

title: The best way to AI-proof your career | Your Turn
description: The best way to AI-proof a career is not to compete with artificial intelligence. It is to develop the skills, capabilities, and sense of purpose that AI cannot replace.
